summary: Solid Zod schema implementation with good test coverage across happy paths and basic rejections. However, pushEnvironmentSchema uses .strict() while all other schemas do not, creating an inconsistency in how extra properties are handled. Several missing edge case tests (missing verdict in devReview, negative scores, negative durations) leave gaps in validation confidence.

suggestions.description: pushEnvironmentSchema uses .strict() but no test verifies that extra properties are rejected
suggestions.rationale: The .strict() modifier on pushEnvironmentSchema (schemas.ts line 85) is untested behavior. This is the only schema using .strict(), making it a distinctive behavior that should have test coverage proving it works as intended. If .strict() were accidentally removed, no test would catch the regression.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test: expect(() => pushEnvironmentSchema.parse({ os: "darwin", extraProp: "value" })).toThrow()

suggestions.description: pushEnvironmentSchema uses .strict() but no other object schema does, creating inconsistent extra-property handling
suggestions.rationale: With .strict() only on pushEnvironmentSchema, extra properties throw there but are silently stripped on sessionSchema, projectEntitySchema, devTaskSchema, and all other object schemas. This inconsistency will confuse consumers who expect uniform behavior across the push payload. Either all schemas should be strict or none should.
suggestions.suggestion: Remove .strict() from pushEnvironmentSchema to match the behavior of all other schemas, or add .strict() consistently to all schemas if rejecting extra properties is desired

suggestions.description: No test for devReviewSchema rejecting missing verdict field
suggestions.rationale: devReviewSchema has two required fields: reviewer and verdict. There is a test for missing reviewer (line 198) but none for missing verdict. Both are required strings; the asymmetry in test coverage leaves the verdict requirement unverified.
suggestions.suggestion: Add: it("rejects missing verdict", () => { expect(() => devReviewSchema.parse({ reviewer: "x" })).toThrow(); })

suggestions.description: No test for negative or boundary scores in qualityDataSchema
suggestions.rationale: qualityDataSchema uses z.number() for score with no min/max constraints. Negative scores (-1), Infinity, and NaN would all parse successfully. If scores should represent percentages or positive values, this is a validation gap. Even if unconstrained numbers are intentional, a test should document that negative scores are accepted.
suggestions.suggestion: Either add z.number().min(0) to the schema and test it, or add a test documenting that negative scores are accepted: expect(qualityDataSchema.parse({ score: -1 })).toEqual({ score: -1 })

suggestions.description: No test for negative duration values in sessionSchema or devTaskSchema
suggestions.rationale: Both sessionSchema.duration and devTaskSchema.duration accept z.number().optional(). A duration of -100 would parse successfully. If durations represent elapsed time, negative values are nonsensical. Like the score issue, this should either be constrained or documented as intentionally unconstrained.
suggestions.suggestion: Either add z.number().nonnegative() to duration fields, or add a test documenting that negative durations are accepted
